title: GROUPING SETS on groups with aliases provided in the SELECT returns empty result
poc: create table students (course TEXT, type TEXT);
insert into students (course, type) values ('CS', 'Bachelor'), ('CS', 'Bachelor'), ('CS', 'PhD'), ('Math', 'Masters'), ('CS', NULL), ('CS', NULL), ('Math', NULL);
select count(*), course, type from students group by grouping sets((course, type), (type)) order by 1, 2, 3;
select count(*), course AS crs, type AS tp from students group by grouping sets((crs, tp), (tp)) order by 1, 2, 3;
key_bug_features: GROUPING SETS with aliases in SELECT returns empty result
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE courses (course_name TEXT, credits INT, department TEXT);
INSERT INTO courses (course_name, credits, department) VALUES ('Database Systems', 3, 'CS'), ('Algorithms', 4, 'CS'), ('Linear Algebra', 3, 'Math'), ('Statistics', 3, NULL);
CREATE TABLE enrollments (student_id INT, course_name TEXT, grade FLOAT);
INSERT INTO enrollments (student_id, course_name, grade) VALUES (1, 'Database Systems', 3.5), (2, 'Algorithms', NULL), (3, 'Linear Algebra', 4.0), (1, 'Statistics', NULL);
SELECT COUNT(*), course_name, department FROM courses GROUP BY GROUPING SETS((course_name, department), (department)) ORDER BY 1, 2, 3;
